title: 2024年度 第1回検定一次試験 英検準1級 リスニング

parts.name: 第1部
parts.description: 対話を聞き、その質問に対して最も適切な答えを1, 2, 3, 4の中から一つ選ぶ形式です。

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Greg, a bunch of the tiles around the sink are coming loose.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: I noticed that. Some of them need to be replaced.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Can you take care of it?

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Yes, but we won't be able to use the sink for a couple of days.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: We only had the kitchen redone two years ago. It seems too soon for this.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Well, there's not really any way to avoid it. It's probably something we'll have to deal with every so often.

questions.question: What does the man imply?
choices: The kitchen repair is not necessary.
The woman is partly to blame.
The sink will have to be replaced.
The problem will occur again.
questions.explanation: 男性はこの問題は避けられず、時々対処しなければならないと言っています。
highlights: It's probably something we'll have to deal with every so often
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「修理不要」→交換が必要。選択肢2「女性のせい」→言及なし。選択肢3「シンクを交換」→タイルの問題。選択肢4「問題は再発する」→正解。

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Hello. I'd like to make a change to my hotel reservation.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: OK. What can I help with?

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: When I made my reservation, I booked my stay for five days starting on April 15, but I need to change that to April 19.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: I can definitely do that. However, there will be an extra charge.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Why is that?

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: There's no charge for changing reservations. But you're shifting from a weekday-only stay to one that goes over the weekend.

questions.question: What does the man tell the woman?
choices: Room fees are higher on weekends.
Weekday bookings are not available.
The reservation cannot be changed.
The hotel has increased all of its prices.
questions.explanation: 平日のみの宿泊から週末にまたがる宿泊に変更するため、追加料金がかかります。
highlights: you're shifting from a weekday-only stay to one that goes over the weekend
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「週末は料金が高い」→正解。選択肢2「平日は予約不可」→言及なし。選択肢3「変更不可」→変更可能。選択肢4「全体的値上げ」→週末の差額。

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Are you ready for your marathon this weekend? I know you've been preparing for months.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Yeah. The weather forecast looks great, and I know you'll be there cheering me on.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: You bet. There'll be places for you to get water, but I'll also give you some energy bars.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Everything will be great as long as my knee doesn't cause problems. It's feeling strong right now, but it hasn't fully recovered from my accident.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: If it's hurting at any point, you should stop.

questions.question: What does the man say about the marathon?
choices: There might not be enough water provided.
It might be canceled because of the weather.
His knee could be an issue.
He did not train enough.
questions.explanation: 男性は膝が事故から完全に回復していないことを心配しています。
highlights: as long as my knee doesn't cause problems
it hasn't fully recovered from my accident
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「水不足」→水は提供される。選択肢2「天気で中止」→天気は良い。選択肢3「膝が問題になるかも」→正解。選択肢4「訓練不足」→数ヶ月準備。

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Good night, Karim. See you in the morning.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Good night. Oh, you drove to the office today, right? Make sure you avoid Highway 402 completely.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Why's that?

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Apparently, there was a major collision near Maple Boulevard.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Are you serious? That's my exit.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Well, you could make a detour on Kent Street instead.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: That's a good idea. It's a few miles longer, but it's better than sitting in traffic.

questions.question: What will the woman likely do?
choices: Go home by a different route from usual.
Drive part of the way on Highway 402.
Avoid driving on Kent Street.
Stay at work until the accident is cleared.
questions.explanation: 女性はKent Streetを迂回路として使い、通常と異なるルートで帰宅します。
highlights: you could make a detour on Kent Street instead
That's a good idea
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「通常と異なるルート」→正解。選択肢2「402号線の一部」→完全に避ける。選択肢3「Kent Streetを避ける」→逆にKent Streetを使う。選択肢4「職場に留まる」→迂回して帰る。

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: I don't like this laundry soap you bought. Our clothes smell strange.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Are you sure it's the detergent? I think we might have mold growing in our machine.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: I never thought of that. Maybe we should wash it out with vinegar or something.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Good idea. I'll check online and see if I can get some tips on how to do it.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: OK. I hope it works. I'd hate to pay a lot of money for a professional cleaner to do it, much less buy a new machine.

questions.question: What do the man and the woman decide to do?
choices: Get information about cleaning the machine.
Look for a new washing machine.
Go back to their previous laundry soap.
Ask someone to repair the machine.
questions.explanation: 男性はオンラインで洗濯機の掃除方法を調べると言っています。
highlights: I'll check online and see if I can get some tips on how to do it
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「掃除の情報を得る」→正解。選択肢2「新しい洗濯機」→避けたい。選択肢3「前の洗剤に戻す」→カビが原因。選択肢4「修理を頼む」→まず自分で掃除。

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Our gym membership is expiring next month. Do you want to renew? I'm pretty sure the cost hasn't changed.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: I don't know. I'm there so infrequently.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: I haven't been there since I started running, so I'm going to let mine expire. You used to take morning classes every week. Have you gotten tired of them?

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: I just haven't been able to find the time lately. I have all those early meetings.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Well, think it over and let me know what you decide.

questions.question: What is one thing the woman says?
choices: She finds the gym classes too tiring.
The gym has become very expensive.
She wants to join a different gym.
She cannot find time to go to the gym.
questions.explanation: 女性は最近時間が見つからないと言っています。朝のミーティングがあるためです。
highlights: I just haven't been able to find the time lately
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「クラスが疲れる」→言及なし。選択肢2「高くなった」→費用は変わっていない。選択肢3「別のジム」→言及なし。選択肢4「時間がない」→正解。

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Dana, can you tidy up, please? Your friends left a huge mess here last night.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Sure, Mark. I'll do it this evening after I get back from my chemistry class.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: I'm having people over to study in the living room this afternoon. I don't think it's too much to ask that we both keep the common areas clean.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: You know I'm busy studying for finals. Can't you just tidy up this time? I'll do it next time, I promise.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: No. I'm not cleaning up for you anymore.

questions.question: Why is the man upset with the woman?
choices: She blamed him for the mess.
She does not want to study with him.
She does not keep the shared space clean.
She stays out late with her friends.
questions.explanation: 男性は女性の友人が大きな散らかしをして、女性が共用スペースを清潔に保っていないことに怒っています。
highlights: Your friends left a huge mess here
we both keep the common areas clean
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「男性のせいにした」→言及なし。選択肢2「一緒に勉強したくない」→言及なし。選択肢3「共用スペースを清潔にしない」→正解。選択肢4「友人と遅くまで」→言及なし。

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Do you know what time the catering company will be bringing the food for lunch during today's workshop?

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: At eleven, but there's been a change in the menu. The lasagna has been replaced by ham sandwiches because of a problem with their ovens. Dessert will still be vanilla ice cream.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: We really need to find a new catering service. We keep having these problems. Last time, they didn't even bring enough food for everyone attending.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: I agree. Let's get through today and then look into other companies.

questions.question: What does the man say about today's lunch?
choices: Hot food will not be served.
The catering company has changed.
Dessert is not included.
It will be served later than planned.
questions.explanation: ラザニア（温かい料理）がオーブンの故障でハムサンドイッチ（冷たい料理）に変更されました。
highlights: The lasagna has been replaced by ham sandwiches because of a problem with their ovens
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「温かい食べ物が出ない」→正解。オーブン故障でサンドイッチに。選択肢2「ケータリング会社が変わった」→まだ変えていない。選択肢3「デザートなし」→アイスクリームあり。選択肢4「遅延」→11時予定。

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Professor Kendall, I went over the comments you left on my paper. Can I ask you some clarifying questions?

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Sure.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: You said we should follow the style guide specified on the course web page. So, I looked up how to make the citations list according to those guidelines. But, from your comment, it sounds like I did something wrong?

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Your citations list is OK, but the body of your paper needs to be spaced differently. And the size of the font is too small. Please use 12-point size.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Ah, OK. Now I understand.

questions.question: What problem did the professor find with the man's paper?
choices: It was about the wrong topic.
It was uploaded to the wrong web page.
It was not formatted correctly.
It did not have good research sources.
questions.explanation: 教授は本文の行間とフォントサイズの問題を指摘しました。フォーマットの問題です。
highlights: the body of your paper needs to be spaced differently
the size of the font is too small
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「トピックが違う」→言及なし。選択肢2「間違ったページにアップ」→言及なし。選択肢3「フォーマットが正しくない」→正解。選択肢4「出典が悪い」→引用リストはOK。

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: You're leaving on vacation soon, right?

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Yeah. Tomorrow is my last day in the office for two weeks! But that reminds me—what's the protocol around here for souvenirs? Am I expected to bring something for everyone? This is my first vacation since I started working here.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: You're so nice to ask! Some people bring back local treats and leave them in the break room for everyone to share.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: OK. I'll stick with food, then. Any dietary restrictions or allergies I should know about?

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Not that I'm aware of.

questions.question: What does the man want to know more about?
choices: Requesting time off from work.
Giving his coworkers gifts.
Traveling with a food allergy.
The woman's upcoming vacation.
questions.explanation: 男性は同僚へのお土産のマナーについて知りたいと思っています。
highlights: what's the protocol around here for souvenirs?
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「休暇申請」→もう決まっている。選択肢2「同僚へのギフト」→正解。選択肢3「アレルギーのある旅」→同僚のアレルギーを聞いた。選択肢4「女性の休暇」→言及なし。

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Ah, Pei-Ling, I'm glad to see you! I can't get into the building, and our meeting is about to start. Do you know the keycode?

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Oh no. I don't have the code either. I'm surprised it's locked. I thought there were night classes in this building.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Me, too. I wonder if the others were able to get in or if they're also locked out but at a different entrance.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: I'll text Kayla. She made the arrangements for the meeting space with the university administrators. She'll know what to do.

questions.question: Why was the man happy to see Pei-Ling?
choices: He wanted to study with her.
He hoped she could open the door.
She had Kayla's phone number.
She planned the meeting he was going to.
questions.explanation: 男性は建物に入れず、Pei-Lingがキーコードを知っているかもしれないと期待しました。
highlights: I can't get into the building
Do you know the keycode?
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「一緒に勉強」→言及なし。選択肢2「ドアを開けてくれると期待」→正解。選択肢3「Kaylaの番号」→後に判明。選択肢4「会議を企画」→Kaylaが企画。

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Will you be joining us for the neighborhood meeting on Friday afternoon, Russell?

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Unfortunately, I don't think so. My kid's school doesn't have an after-school program, so I have to pick her up at four o'clock sharp.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: That's too bad. You bring such a valuable perspective with your past work experience in the city government.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: I could come late, but I don't know how long I'd be able to stay. My daughter will get impatient—she's only six.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Maybe we should consider providing childcare for our meetings. Then, more neighbors with children might come.

questions.question: What do we learn about the man?
choices: He is good at looking after children.
He is new to the neighborhood.
He led the meeting last Friday.
He used to work for the local government.
questions.explanation: 女性は男性の市政府での過去の経験が貴重な視点をもたらすと言っています。
highlights: your past work experience in the city government
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「子供の世話が上手」→言及なし。選択肢2「新しい住民」→言及なし。選択肢3「前回の会議を主導」→言及なし。選択肢4「市政府で働いていた」→正解。

parts.name: 第2部
parts.description: 英文を聞き、その質問に対して最も適切な答えを1, 2, 3, 4の中から一つ選ぶ形式です。

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: Swarms of grasshoppers known as locusts have been notorious throughout history for completely destroying crops. In a single day, a relatively small group of these insects can eat through enough food to feed tens of thousands of people. Despite attempts to control them with pesticides, locusts travel and reproduce quickly and are unpredictable in their movements, so success has been limited. However, locusts are known to even eat each other, and scientists see promise in exploiting this tendency.

questions.question: What does the speaker imply about pesticides?
choices: They are not able to fully control locusts.
They are not safe to put on crops.
Better ones may soon be developed.
Their effects are often difficult to predict.
questions.explanation: 殺虫剤でバッタを制御しようとしても成功は限られていると言っています。
highlights: Despite attempts to control them with pesticides...success has been limited
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「完全に制御できない」→正解。選択肢2「作物に安全でない」→言及なし。選択肢3「改良される」→言及なし。選択肢4「予測困難」→バッタの動きが予測困難。

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: As locust swarms develop, the insects emit a chemical that makes them less likely to eat one another. This enables the group to grow without destroying itself. Researchers experimented by editing locust DNA. When locusts were created without the ability to smell this chemical, their likelihood of consuming other locusts increased. Similarly, locusts that were engineered not to produce the chemical were more likely to become another's meal. Scientists hope these techniques can reduce or eliminate destructive locusts someday.

questions.question: What do we learn about the chemical produced by locusts?
choices: It prevents locusts from killing each other.
It increases locusts' ability to smell food sources.
It can cause locust swarms to break up.
It causes locusts to quickly grow in size.
questions.explanation: この化学物質はバッタ同士が食べ合うことを防ぎ、群れを大きく成長させます。
highlights: emit a chemical that makes them less likely to eat one another
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「共食いを防ぐ」→正解。選択肢2「食料源の嗅覚を強化」→言及なし。選択肢3「群れを解散させる」→逆に成長を助ける。選択肢4「急速に成長」→群れの成長。

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: Golden-shouldered parrots once inhabited northern Australia in large numbers, but today, only two small populations remain. While invasive predators are often to blame when Australian animals become endangered, the parrots face a different threat—native tea trees. The trees grow in the parrots' grassland habitat, where they block the birds' view of approaching predators. By the time the parrots notice the attack, it is often too late for them to escape.

questions.question: Why have golden-shouldered parrots' numbers decreased?
choices: They are unable to run away from predators.
The grasses they consume are disappearing.
Other birds are using their nests.
Other animal species are increasing in number.
questions.explanation: 茶の木が捕食者の接近を見えなくし、逃げるのが遅れます。
highlights: they block the birds' view of approaching predators
it is often too late for them to escape
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「捕食者から逃げられない」→正解。選択肢2「草が消滅」→茶の木が生えている。選択肢3「巣を使われる」→言及なし。選択肢4「他の種が増加」→言及なし。

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: The tea trees' growth is the result of cattle farms, which increased throughout the 1900s and forced indigenous peoples off their lands. These peoples had managed the grasslands, and the shade provided by the grasses prevented tea-tree growth. The loss of this land management, along with the cattle's consumption of grass, allowed the trees to spread. Efforts to clear the trees are underway, but obtaining government approval to destroy native vegetation can be difficult and time-consuming. For now, the parrots' future remains uncertain.

questions.question: What is one thing the speaker says about the tea trees?
choices: Shady conditions do not prevent their growth.
They have prevented cattle farms from expanding.
The government has tried to plant more of them.
It is not easy to get rid of them.
questions.explanation: 茶の木を除去するには在来植生を破壊する政府の許可が必要で、困難で時間がかかります。
highlights: obtaining government approval to destroy native vegetation can be difficult and time-consuming
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「日陰でも成長」→日陰で成長が抑えられていた。選択肢2「牧場の拡大を防いだ」→逆に牧場が茶の木を増やした。選択肢3「政府が植えた」→言及なし。選択肢4「除去が難しい」→正解。

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: Recently, establishing a colony on Mars has been considered by some as a near-future goal for humanity. However, there are many major hurdles to such a task. Aside from the technical innovations required to build a sustainable habitat, such a long journey by spacecraft itself raises serious problems. Much equipment would be necessary, but it can currently cost tens of thousands of dollars to lift a single kilogram into space. The European Space Agency published a study suggesting a way to maximize efficiency.

questions.question: What does the speaker imply about space travel?
choices: Astronauts frequently need to resupply.
Its funding has been increased recently.
It is difficult to bring everything needed.
It is receiving little public support.
questions.explanation: 1kgを宇宙に持ち上げるのに数万ドルかかるため、必要なものを全て持っていくのが困難です。
highlights: it can currently cost tens of thousands of dollars to lift a single kilogram into space
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「補給が必要」→言及なし。選択肢2「資金が増加」→言及なし。選択肢3「必要なものを運ぶのが困難」→正解。選択肢4「国民の支持が少ない」→言及なし。

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: Based on its calculations, all-female crews may be much more efficient. Due to their smaller average size, a four-female crew would need about 1,700 kilograms less food than men for a 1,080-day mission, saving $158 million. Additionally, smaller bodies use less energy, so they consume less water and oxygen and produce less carbon dioxide. On long missions, seemingly small differences could result in big advantages.

questions.question: What is one thing we learn about male astronauts?
choices: They are smaller than average men.
They need more resources than female astronauts.
They are better suited for long missions.
They make up the majority of astronauts.
questions.explanation: 男性は女性より体が大きく、食料、水、酸素をより多く消費します。
highlights: a four-female crew would need about 1,700 kilograms less food than men
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「平均より小さい」→言及なし。選択肢2「女性より多くのリソースが必要」→正解。選択肢3「長期ミッションに適す」→逆に非効率。選択肢4「多数派」→言及なし。

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: The Oxus civilization was a Bronze Age society located in Central Asia. However, despite several archaeological sites being discovered that contain structures and various artifacts, compared to many other Bronze Age civilizations, much remains unknown about it. Scholars are not even sure how far the civilization extended. They are determined to learn more about the ancient society, though, and recent research has produced various theories about its history.

questions.question: What is one thing that we learn about the Oxus civilization?
choices: It may have influenced cultures across much of Asia.
None of its buildings have been found.
It lasted longer than other civilizations.
It is not well understood by researchers.
questions.explanation: 他の青銅器時代の文明と比べて、多くのことがまだ分かっていません。
highlights: much remains unknown about it
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「アジア中に影響」→言及なし。選択肢2「建物がない」→構造物が発見されている。選択肢3「長く続いた」→言及なし。選択肢4「よく理解されていない」→正解。

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: Artifacts uncovered by archaeologists suggest that the society underwent major changes before vanishing. Objects that indicated social status seemed to have stopped being used, suggesting a shift in the power structure. Therefore, some scholars think the civilization's fall may have been sparked by social and political changes. Others have a different theory. The region in which the civilization was located was extremely dry. Some researchers argue that increasing desertification and other environmentally related factors were responsible for the disappearance of the Oxus society.

questions.question: What is one thing researchers disagree about regarding the Oxus civilization?
choices: How much power it had in the region.
How much importance it placed on social status.
What caused it to eventually disappear.
What environmental problems it had.
questions.explanation: 一部は社会政治的変化が原因と考え、他の研究者は砂漠化などの環境要因が原因と主張しています。
highlights: some scholars think the civilization's fall may have been sparked by social and political changes
Others have a different theory
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「地域での権力」→言及なし。選択肢2「社会的地位の重要性」→言及なし。選択肢3「消滅の原因」→正解。選択肢4「環境問題の内容」→消滅の原因について。

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: Popular cities for international tourists, such as London or Paris, often have world-famous museums. Nukus, however, is a lesser-known city in a remote area of Uzbekistan. Despite the city's smaller tourism industry, the Nukus Museum of Art has one of the world's foremost collections of avant-garde art. The museum's founder, Igor Savitsky, assembled his collection in the desert city of Nukus for a good reason.

questions.question: What is one thing we learn about the city of Nukus?
choices: It was founded by Igor Savitsky.
It receives relatively few tourists.
It was once the capital of Uzbekistan.
It is home to many famous artists.
questions.explanation: ヌクスはあまり知られておらず、観光産業が小さい都市です。
highlights: Nukus, however, is a lesser-known city
the city's smaller tourism industry
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「Savitskyが設立」→彼は美術館の設立者。選択肢2「観光客が少ない」→正解。選択肢3「首都だった」→言及なし。選択肢4「有名な芸術家の本拠地」→言及なし。

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: Because of the severe political situation at the time, much of the art was banned when Savitsky collected it. In fact, only art in the style of Soviet realism was allowed. However, Savitsky was able to acquire tens of thousands of forbidden works because of his determination and because of Nukus's location. Government authorities far away in Tashkent and Moscow were largely unaware of his actions. These days, the government recognizes the value of the collection, but managing it effectively in such a location is a challenge.

questions.question: What is unique about much of the art owned by the Nukus Museum?
choices: It contains strong political themes.
It was all created by the same man.
It was not allowed by the government.
It is in the style of Soviet realism.
questions.explanation: コレクションの多くは収集時に政府に禁じられていた前衛芸術でした。
highlights: much of the art was banned when Savitsky collected it
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「政治的テーマ」→言及なし。選択肢2「同じ人が作成」→様々なアーティスト。選択肢3「政府に禁じられていた」→正解。選択肢4「ソ連リアリズム」→それだけが許可されていた。

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: In many places, it is illegal to let lawns or other grassy areas become overgrown. However, while most people are striving to reduce their carbon dioxide emissions, mechanical lawn mowers are generally powered by fossil fuels. This means property owners often disregard these environmental concerns. Now, though, a low-tech solution is catching on in areas across the United States: "rent-a-goat" services. These bring herds of goats to remove excess grass simply by eating it.

questions.question: What is one problem with requiring that people cut their grass?
choices: People rarely cooperate with it.
It may cause an increase in pollution.
It unfairly targets the technology industry.
There are many areas that need more grass.
questions.explanation: 芝刈り機は化石燃料で動くため、芝を刈ることは環境汚染を増やす可能性があります。
highlights: mechanical lawn mowers are generally powered by fossil fuels
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「協力しない」→言及なし。選択肢2「汚染の増加」→正解。選択肢3「技術産業を標的」→言及なし。選択肢4「草が必要な地域」→言及なし。

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: The goats are effective at removing unwanted vegetation. They also have an easy time navigating rough terrain, such as rocky hillsides. Businesses and property owners that have rented goat herds to help maintain their lawns also report increased interest from the community. The unusual sight of a herd of goats in locations like city parks can turn them into temporary attractions. These are popular with local families, who bring their children to watch them work.

questions.question: What is one benefit of using goats to remove grass?
choices: They are a source of entertainment for communities.
They prevent areas from becoming overly rocky.
They can boost the productivity of gardens.
They are less likely to cause harm to children.
questions.explanation: ヤギの群れは一時的なアトラクションとなり、地域の家族に人気です。
highlights: turn them into temporary attractions
popular with local families
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「コミュニティの娯楽」→正解。選択肢2「岩場を防ぐ」→岩場を歩ける。選択肢3「庭の生産性」→言及なし。選択肢4「子供に安全」→子供が見に来る。

parts.name: 第3部
parts.description: 現実の場面を想定したリスニング問題です。音声を聞き、最も適切な答えを選んでください。

questions.situation: Your family is expecting guests today. You used the blue towels earlier in the day, and they are now dirty. Your mother tells you the following.

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: Can you get the guest bedroom ready? Please take the used bedding off and wash it. You don't need to wait to remake the bed, though—there are spare sheets and a quilt in the closet. Make the bed with them while the other bedding is being washed. Oh, before you start the washing machine, can you throw in the dirty towels from the bathroom? If the nice blue towels are clean, hang them in the bathroom. If not, wait until the laundry is dry, then put them out.

questions.question: What should you do last?
choices: Make the bed with clean bedding.
Start the washing machine.
Clean the bathroom.
Hang the towels back up.
questions.explanation: 青いタオルは汚れている→洗濯が乾くまで待つ→乾いたら掛ける。これが最後の作業。
highlights: If not, wait until the laundry is dry, then put them out
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「ベッドメイク」→洗濯中にする。選択肢2「洗濯機を回す」→途中の作業。選択肢3「バスルーム掃除」→言及なし。選択肢4「タオルを掛ける」→正解。乾いた後で最後に。

questions.situation: You will play a video game for the first time. Your friend offers to teach you. You have already logged in. Your friend tells you the following.

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: The first step is to set up a user profile and then use that profile to log in. Once you're logged in, you have to choose if you want to play single-player or with others. Since you'll be playing with me, choose "two players." There are also two different modes of play—"building," which is just creating new worlds, and "competing," which is fighting against characters in the game. Let's do "building," OK? I think it's more fun and easier to learn.

questions.question: What should you do next?
choices: Create a user profile.
Choose the number of players.
Select the mode of play.
Start building a new world.
questions.explanation: 既にログイン済み→次はプレイヤー数を選択（「two players」を選ぶ）。
highlights: Once you're logged in, you have to choose if you want to play single-player or with others
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「プロフィール作成」→ログイン前の手順で完了済み。選択肢2「プレイヤー数を選択」→正解。ログイン後の次のステップ。選択肢3「モード選択」→プレイヤー数の後。選択肢4「世界を作る」→ゲーム開始後。

questions.situation: You are traveling and need to go from the airport to the Regal Hotel. You want to take the route with the shortest walking distance. An information counter employee tells you the following.

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: The Regal Hotel? OK, you have a few choices. The Silver Arrow is a direct bus from the airport to Downtown Center, which is a four-block walk to the Regal. Next, it's a bit farther—six blocks, I think—from the commuter train station where the Metro South Line ends, Lincoln Terminal. If you don't mind taking the subway, the Regal Hotel is situated between two stations. You can take the Orange Line to Standish Street Station and walk four blocks, or the Blue Line to Franklin Square, which is about two blocks. Hope you have a good stay!

questions.question: How should you get to the hotel?
choices: Take the Silver Arrow.
Take the Metro South Line.
Take the Orange Line.
Take the Blue Line.
questions.explanation: 最短徒歩距離→Silver Arrow: 4ブロック、Metro South: 6ブロック、Orange Line: 4ブロック、Blue Line: 2ブロック→Blue Lineが最短。
highlights: the Blue Line to Franklin Square, which is about two blocks
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「Silver Arrow」→4ブロック。選択肢2「Metro South」→6ブロック。選択肢3「Orange Line」→4ブロック。選択肢4「Blue Line」→正解。2ブロックで最短。

questions.situation: You are shopping for a new computer. You want a small, light laptop, but you are not interested in a tablet. You hear the following announcement.

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: Attention, shoppers. Today only, there are special savings on the following electronics. Our most powerful desktop computer, the Lightning 500, perfect for gaming or design work, is 20 percent off. Is ease of portability your main concern? You can't beat a tablet! Pick up a Slick Assistant for only $200! If you prefer a computer instead of a tablet but still want it to be portable, the Wizard 50 laptop fits easily into most bags. And a free two-year warranty is included. Smart TVs are on sale, too—check out the BRR Quartz. It's perfect for streaming your favorite movies!

questions.question: Which item should you buy?
choices: The Lightning 500.
The Slick Assistant.
The Wizard 50.
The BRR Quartz.
questions.explanation: 小さくて軽いラップトップが欲しい＆タブレットは不要→Wizard 50がバッグに入るポータブルなラップトップ。
highlights: the Wizard 50 laptop fits easily into most bags
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「Lightning 500」→デスクトップ。選択肢2「Slick Assistant」→タブレット。選択肢3「Wizard 50」→正解。ポータブルラップトップ。選択肢4「BRR Quartz」→スマートTV。

questions.situation: Your company will host a social event for clients. You have to choose the site. There will be three hundred guests, and outdoors is preferred. An event planner tells you the following.

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: There are various options available. The cheapest option is Wingra Hills. There's an outdoor pavilion, but parking is limited—only about thirty cars, and there's no public transportation, unfortunately. Charlevoix Manor is a lovely old stone building that looks like a castle. The ballroom is on the small side, but it's gorgeous. Their catering is good, too. How about Park Tower? The rooftop garden has great views—you'd have to keep the guest list under two hundred, though. Or there's the Ozark Terrace. It's a lovely garden space adjacent to a lake, and it can accommodate up to five hundred people.

questions.question: Which site should you choose?
choices: Wingra Hills.
Charlevoix Manor.
Park Tower.
The Ozark Terrace.
questions.explanation: 300人のゲスト＆屋外が望ましい→Wingra Hills: 駐車場が限られすぎ。Charlevoix Manor: 室内の舞踏室。Park Tower: 200人未満。Ozark Terrace: 湖沿いの庭園で500人まで→最適。
highlights: the Ozark Terrace
a lovely garden space adjacent to a lake
it can accommodate up to five hundred people
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「Wingra Hills」→駐車場30台で300人は不可能。選択肢2「Charlevoix Manor」→室内で小さい。選択肢3「Park Tower」→200人未満制限。選択肢4「Ozark Terrace」→正解。庭園で500人まで。
